Comment: Long-running asynchronous job orchestration. SubmitJob starts an external job, then the execution loops over a Wait state and a poll Task with exponential backoff so it consumes no compute while the job runs (executions may span minutes to days). On success the workflow pauses on a task-token Task (.waitForTaskToken) for human-in-the-loop sign-off, resuming only when SendTaskSuccess is called with the token. Polls exhaust the budget -> JobPollBudgetExhausted; no sign-off before the timeout -> SignoffTimedOut.

SubmitJob.Comment: Start the async job and persist its initial state. The result replaces the execution input so every later state reads from $.job.

WaitForJob.Comment: Idle wait between polls. SecondsPath lets the poller tune the next interval (exponential backoff up to a cap), so the execution holds no compute while the job runs.

PollJob.Comment: Check the job's status and recompute the next backed-off wait interval.

EvaluateJob.Comment: Branch on the polled status: succeed -> sign-off gate, fail -> Fail, poll budget exhausted -> Fail, otherwise wait and poll again.

RequestSignoff.Comment: Human-in-the-loop gate. The Lambda is handed the task token and records it (and would notify an operator) so the job can be approved out-of-band. The execution pauses here until SendTaskSuccess (or SendTaskFailure) is called with the token.

Finalize.Comment: Terminal happy-path bookkeeping: mark the job COMPLETED.

JobFailed.Cause: The long-running job failed during submission, polling, or processing.

JobTimedOut.Cause: The job did not reach a terminal status within the configured poll budget.

SignoffTimedOut.Cause: No sign-off callback (task token) was received before the timeout.
